answer: Yes, do your easy run — breathing hard at the end of a sprint is your body working through the effort, and feeling normal by the walk is the expected pattern. Your data doesn't include a recovery or HRV reading, so I can't tell you this run is safe to repeat, but the pattern matches your training, and that's the useful signal. Run as planned, and if the easy part feels unusually heavy or your legs feel unusually heavy the next day, make tomorrow's run an easy day instead of a hard one.
